name: Melatonin
summary: Decision-ready summary: Melatonin is best framed for sleep-onset latency; jet lag; circadian rhythm shift; safety screen: sedatives; pregnancy without clinician guidance; autoimmune conditions review.
description: Melatonin supports sleep onset and circadian alignment; may reduce jet lag and shift phase by acting on melatonin receptor pathways.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: Melatonin may help sleep timing/onset when dose and timing are matched to the sleep problem.
claimMap.notes: Melatonin may help sleep timing/onset when dose and timing are matched to the sleep problem.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: effect peaked around 4 mg/day; timing around 3 h before desired bedtime in meta-regression
qualifiers.population: Patients with insomnia and healthy volunteers

claimMap.claim: consistent reduction in sleep onset latency
claimMap.notes: consistent reduction in sleep onset latency

claimMap.claim: Higher-dose melatonin did not show a detectable SAE increase in a limited low-risk subset but increased drowsiness/headache/dizziness; safety reporting was limited.
claimMap.notes: Higher-dose melatonin did not show a detectable SAE increase in a limited low-risk subset but increased drowsiness/headache/dizziness; safety reporting was limited.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: high dose defined as ≥10 mg in included trials

sources.title: Optimizing the Time and Dose of Melatonin as a Sleep-Promoting Drug: A Systematic Review of Randomized Controlled Trials and Dose-Response Meta-Analysis
sources.authors: F. Cruz-Sanabria; S. Bruno; A. Crippa; P. Frumento; M. Scarselli; Debra J. Skene; U. Faraguna
sources.note: melatonin dose/timing and sleep-onset runtime rules
